user_query:
I want to institutionalize a quiet, breakpoint-based self-critique layer for our upcoming tenant onboarding web portal project. Please read the inputs under input/ and produce a small, usable package under output/ that we can adopt immediately.

Inputs to read:
- input/project_plan.md — phased plan and major workstreams
- input/constraints.md — risk tolerance and depth preferences
- input/feedback_log.jsonl — real incidents from prior projects that caused rework

Deliverables to write:
1) output/memory.md — a compact trigger memory using the structure in the input files: include Status (status, version, last), Context (situations, risk signals, preferred depths), and Notes (confirmed trigger rules and candidate checkpoints). Keep it outcome-focused and aligned to the constraints.
2) output/checkpoints.md — named breakpoint prompts with two sections: Confirmed and Candidates. In Confirmed, add at least 3 checkpoints mapped to phases in input/project_plan.md and explicitly label the Trigger type (before branching, before commitment, after user friction, after surprising evidence, or at risky handoffs), the chosen Depth (light|standard|deep), and 1–2 hard questions to ask. In Candidates, include at least 1 item that you think we should validate later.
3) output/incidents.md — add at least one incident derived from input/feedback_log.jsonl using a short entry with fields: Miss, Late catch, Better breakpoint, and a Promote flag (no|candidate|yes). Only log reusable lessons.
4) output/plan_with_breakpoints.md — copy the core phases from input/project_plan.md and insert clear, short "=== Breakpoint" blocks at minimum 3 inflection points (e.g., before branching into subtasks, before irreversible edits, and before a risky handoff). In each block, include 1–3 focused questions and the chosen Depth, and rewrite/clarify any phase plan if your critique reveals a framing issue.
5) output/steering_snippets.md — optional steering text we could paste into our workspace docs to keep this habit alive (do not modify anything outside output/); keep it short and non-ceremonial.

Ground rules:
- Choose the lightest critique that can still catch the likely failure.
- If a checkpoint invalidates the current frame, adjust the plan in output/plan_with_breakpoints.md before continuing.
- Keep logs tight: save reusable insertion points and avoid noisy narratives.
- Do not write or reference any paths outside output/.

When you’re done, briefly list what you changed in the plan (if anything) at the bottom of output/plan_with_breakpoints.md.
